The mean and standard deviation of a distribution of test scores are \(m\) and \(s\) respectively. If \(4\) marks are added to each score of the distribution, what are the mean and standard deviation of the new distribution?
Mean Standard Deviation

The graph shows the frequency curves of two symmetric distributions \(P\) and \(Q\).

Which of the following is/are true?
I. The mean of \(P \lt \) the mean of \(Q\).
II. The mode of \(P \gt \) the mode of \(Q\).
III. The inter-quartile range of \(P \lt \) the inter-quartile range of \(Q\).
A fair die is thrown \(3\) times. The probability that "6" occurs exactly once is

Which one of the following shaded regions represents the solution of
\(\begin{cases}2 \le x+y \le 6 \\ 0 \le x \le 4 \\ 0 \le y \le 4\end{cases}\)?
If the sum to \(n\) terms of an A.P. is \(n^2+3n\), find the \(7\)th term of the A.P.

If \(x, y, z\) are in G.P., which of the following must be true?
I. \(x+3, y+3, z+3\) are in G.P.
II. \(3x, 3y, 3z\) are in G.P.
III. \(x^2, y^2, z^2\) are in G.P.
\(3\text{ kg}\) of a solution contains \(40\%\) of alcohol by weight. How much alcohol should be added to obtain a solution containing \(50\%\) of alcohol by weight?
\(P\) sold an article to \(Q\) at a profit of \(25\%\). \(Q\) sold it to \(R\) also at a profit of \(25\%\). If \(Q\) gained \( \$ 500 \), how much did \(P\) gain?
From a rectangular metal sheet of width \(3\text{ cm}\) and length \(40\text{ cm}\), at most how many circles each of radius \(1\text{ cm}\) can be cut?
